metadata.description: Reference database of offensive and defensive munitions in the Iran-Israel conflict

offensive.name: Emad
offensive.description: Iran's first precision-guided MRBM based on the Shahab-3 airframe. Features a maneuverable reentry vehicle (MaRV) with a redesigned fin-guided nose cone for improved terminal accuracy. Liquid-fueled single-stage missile with GNSS-aided INS midcourse guidance.

offensive.name: Ghadr
offensive.description: Extended-range Shahab-3 derivative with stretched propellant tanks and a distinctive 'baby bottle' triconic reentry vehicle. Multiple variants (Qadr S/H/F) offer different range-payload trade-offs. Faster 30-minute setup time compared to original Shahab-3.

offensive.name: Kheibar Shekan
offensive.hypersonic_notes: Iran claims Mach 7-10 terminal velocity but this is standard ballistic reentry speed, not sustained hypersonic flight
offensive.basing: Road-mobile; dual-launch TEL on commercial 10-wheel truck; underground tunnel bases
offensive.description: Iran's smallest and most concealable MRBM, weighing one-third of comparable missiles. Features a triconic MaRV reentry vehicle for terminal maneuver stability. Designed for rapid deployment from underground tunnel bases on dual-launch TELs using commercial truck chassis.

offensive.name: Fattah
offensive.cep_notes: Fattah-1 estimate; if confirmed, highest precision in Iranian MRBM inventory
offensive.hypersonic_notes: Fattah-1 is MaRV-MRBM per IISS (>Mach 5 terminal but no glide vehicle). Fattah-2 is a genuine HGV with sustained Mach 5+ glide and lateral maneuvering.

fattah_2.name: Fattah-2
fattah_2.notes: Genuine HGV second stage with lateral maneuvering in glide phase. Iran claims Mach 15 (unverified).

offensive.lineage: Indigenous Iranian design; second-stage sustainer based on Arash-24
offensive.description: Iran's most advanced missile family. Fattah-1 is a two-stage solid-fueled MRBM with MaRV capable of evasive maneuvers up to 100 km before impact using thrust vector control. Fattah-2 carries a genuine hypersonic glide vehicle (HGV) with unpredictable approach vectors, at the cost of reduced payload.

offensive.name: Khorramshahr-4
offensive.range_km_notes: IRGC claims up to 3000 km with reduced warhead; 2000 km with full 1000 kg payload
offensive.warhead_kg_notes: Heaviest warhead in Iranian MRBM inventory; reduced to ~1000 kg for max range
offensive.mirv_notes: IRGC claims MIRV capability (4th generation); unverified by Western sources
offensive.lineage: Khorramshahr family; Shahab-3 lineage via BM-25 Musudan technology
offensive.description: Iran's heaviest-payload MRBM, carrying up to 1500 kg. Fourth-generation Khorramshahr with claimed MIRV capability. Longest-range liquid-fueled MRBM in active Iranian service. First used in combat during True Promise 4 operations.

offensive.name: Sejjil
offensive.guidance_notes: Upgraded guidance from 2021 may include satellite corrections but unconfirmed
offensive.marv_notes: Unconfirmed by CSIS/IISS; some reports claim MaRV but not corroborated
offensive.description: Iran's first two-stage solid-fueled MRBM and longest-range solid-fuel missile in the inventory. Indigenous design (not derived from foreign missiles). Faster launch preparation than liquid-fueled predecessors due to pre-fueled solid motors. Two variants: Sejjil-1 and improved Sejjil-2.

offensive.name: Shahed-136
offensive.range_km_notes: Conservative estimate 970 km; commonly cited 1800 km; high estimate 2500 km
offensive.guidance_notes: Pre-programmed waypoints; highly vulnerable to GPS jamming
offensive.first_combat_use_notes: Abqaiq-Khurais attack; extensively used in Ukraine from Sept 2022
offensive.launch_method: Truck-mounted multiple-rail RATO launcher; fired in salvos
offensive.description: Delta-wing one-way attack drone with pusher propeller, designed for saturation attacks exploiting unfavorable cost-exchange ratios against defenders. At ~$20,000 per unit, forces defenders to expend interceptors costing $50K-$36M. Widely proliferated to Russia (Geran-2), Houthis, and Hezbollah.

offensive.name: Shahed-131
offensive.description: Lighter and shorter-range variant of the Shahed-136 with a smaller 15 kg warhead. Used in saturation attacks alongside Shahed-136 to overwhelm air defenses. Known as Geran-1 in Russian service.

offensive.name: Shahed-238
offensive.warhead_kg_notes: Some estimates up to 85 kg depending on variant
offensive.guidance_notes: Three seeker variants: GPS-only, infrared terminal, radio-frequency terminal. IR and RF variants reduce GNSS dependence.
offensive.first_combat_use_notes: Used by Russia against Ukraine; used by Iran in 2026 operations
offensive.description: Jet-powered evolution of the Shahed-136 family, approximately 2.7x faster with a turbojet engine. Shares the Shahed-136 airframe for logistics commonality. Available in three seeker variants (GPS, IR, RF) for different target types. Flight ceiling comparable to combat aircraft at ~30,000 ft.

offensive.name: Shahed-107
offensive.range_km_notes: Operational radius; some sources cite 150 km radius
offensive.launch_method: Rail launcher / catapult; parachute or belly recovery
offensive.description: Small reconnaissance and strike drone derived from the Ababil-2 family. Can be configured for ISR with a camera payload or as a loitering munition. Compact and road-mobile with catapult launch capability.

offensive.name: Shahed-129
offensive.range_km_notes: Operational range; combat radius approximately 500 km with weapon payload
offensive.warhead_kg_notes: Maximum payload capacity; can carry Sadid-345 PGMs
offensive.lineage: Indigenous Iranian MALE UAV design; resembles Israeli Hermes 450/900
offensive.description: Iran's primary medium-altitude long-endurance (MALE) armed reconnaissance UAV with 24-hour endurance. Resembles the Israeli Hermes 450 in configuration. Can carry Sadid-345 precision-guided munitions for strike missions. Extensively used in Syria and Iraq for ISR and close air support.

offensive.name: Mohajer-6
offensive.lineage: Mohajer family (dating to Iran-Iraq War); 6th generation
offensive.description: Iran's primary medium-altitude ISR and strike UCAV with 12-hour endurance. Carries two Qaem precision-guided munitions under-wing. Exported to Ethiopia and widely used in regional proxy conflicts. Features EO/IR sensor ball for real-time reconnaissance and target designation.

defensive.name: Arrow-3
defensive.description: Top-tier layer of Israeli ballistic missile defense. Intercepts targets in space (exo-atmospheric) before reentry, using kinetic hit-to-kill with no explosive warhead. Guided by the Super Green Pine radar with ~800 km detection range. Successfully intercepted Iranian MRBMs during True Promise operations.

defensive.name: Arrow-2
defensive.description: Primary Israeli BMD interceptor for medium-range threats in the upper endoatmosphere. Uses a blast-fragmentation warhead rather than kinetic kill. Complements Arrow-3 by catching missiles that penetrate the exo-atmospheric layer. Guided by the Green Pine phased-array radar.

defensive.name: David's Sling
defensive.description: Middle layer of Israeli air defense between Iron Dome and Arrow. Uses the Stunner (SkyCeptor) interceptor with a dual-pulse rocket motor and multi-mode seeker combining infrared imaging (IIR) and active radar for hit-to-kill engagement. Designed to counter tactical ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, and large-caliber rockets.

defensive.name: Iron Dome
defensive.unit_cost_notes: Estimated $40K-$50K per Tamir interceptor; each battery has 3-4 launchers with 20 Tamir each
defensive.description: Lowest layer of Israeli air defense and the world's most combat-proven missile defense system. Uses the Tamir interceptor with proximity-fused fragmentation warhead. Each battery covers a city-sized area using the EL/M-2084 radar for tracking and trajectory prediction. Selectively engages only threats headed for populated areas.

defensive.name: THAAD
defensive.description: US Army terminal-phase anti-ballistic missile system deployed to Israel from October 2024. Operates alongside Arrow to provide redundant upper-tier intercept capability in the endo/exo-atmospheric boundary. AN/TPY-2 X-band radar provides ~1000 km detection range. Kinetic hit-to-kill interceptor with no explosive warhead.

defensive.name: Patriot PAC-3
defensive.description: Primary US Army air and missile defense system. PAC-3 variant uses hit-to-kill kinetic interceptor; PAC-2 GEM+ uses blast-fragmentation warhead. PAC-3 MSE (Missile Segment Enhancement) extends range and altitude. Deployed at US Gulf bases and in Israel for point defense against tactical ballistic missiles.

defensive.name: Aegis BMD / SM-3
defensive.intercept_range_km_notes: SM-3 Block IIA; earlier blocks ~700 km
defensive.first_combat_use_notes: USS Carney and other Arleigh Burke-class destroyers intercepted Iranian BMs during True Promise 1
defensive.unit_cost_notes: Estimated $28-36M per SM-3 Block IIA; most expensive interceptor used in this conflict
defensive.platform: Arleigh Burke-class destroyers (DDG-51) and Ticonderoga-class cruisers (CG-47)
defensive.description: Sea-based exo-atmospheric ballistic missile defense launched from Aegis-equipped warships. SM-3 three-stage interceptor uses a Lightweight Exo-Atmospheric Projectile (LEAP) kinetic warhead for hit-to-kill in space. SM-3 Block IIA variant has extended range of ~2500 km. Used by USS Carney and other destroyers against Iranian ballistic missiles during True Promise operations.
